source_constraint: All facts must be verifiable against the MystenLabs/walrus-sites repo portal/ directory.

evals.prompt: How do I set up the local portal to view my testnet Walrus Site?
evals.expected_output: Clone MystenLabs/walrus-sites, cd portal, bun install, copy testnet config, bun -F server start.
expectations: Instructs to clone MystenLabs/walrus-sites (not a separate portal repo)
Instructs to install bun if not present
Instructs to run bun install in the portal/ directory
Instructs to copy portal-config.testnet.example.yaml to portal-config.yaml
Warns NOT to change original_package_id in the config
Shows the start command: bun -F server start (from the portal directory)
Mentions the URL format: <base36-id>.localhost:3000

evals.prompt: What is original_package_id in the portal config? Should I set it to my app's package ID?
evals.expected_output: It's the Walrus Sites framework package (site::Site type), NOT the user's app package. Don't change it.
expectations: Clearly states it is the Walrus Sites framework package, not the user's app package
States the testnet example config already has the correct value
Warns that changing it will break all site lookups
Explains how to verify: sui client object shows objType with the correct package prefix
Does NOT instruct the user to change this value

evals.prompt: I deployed my site to Walrus Sites on testnet but when I visit the URL it shows 'Page not found'. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic checklist: check blob expiration, verify portal config, check if site object ID changed.
expectations: Suggests running site-builder sitemap to check blob expiration dates
Explains that blobs may have expired if --epochs was too low
Mentions checking original_package_id in the portal config
Mentions that re-publishing creates a new site object with a different URL
Does NOT suggest changing original_package_id to the user's app package ID

evals.prompt: The portal won't start because something else is using port 3000. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Find and kill the conflicting process with lsof, then restart the portal.
expectations: Provides lsof -i :3000 or equivalent to identify the conflicting process
Shows how to kill the process
Mentions common conflicts: Vite dev server, Next.js, other Node processes
Instructs to restart the portal after freeing the port

evals.prompt: Can I use my testnet Walrus Site URL on wal.app?
evals.expected_output: No. wal.app is mainnet only. Self-host a local portal for testnet.
expectations: Clearly states wal.app is mainnet only
Directs the user to self-host a local portal for testnet
Does NOT provide a wal.app URL for testnet sites
